Apple___Apple_scab.description: Apple scab is a fungal disease that affects apple trees, causing dark, scabby lesions on leaves and fruit.
organic: Remove and destroy fallen leaves in autumn to reduce spore survival.
Prune trees to improve air circulation.
Apply sulfur-based organic fungicides early in the season.
chemical: Copper-based sprays during the dormant season.

Apple___Black_rot.description: Black rot causes frog-eye leaf spots and cankers on branches, leading to fruit decay.
organic: Prune out dead wood and mummified fruit.
Increase spacing between trees for better airflow.
chemical: Thiophanate-methyl or Captan sprays starting at silver tip stage.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.description: A fungal disease that requires both apple trees and Junipers to complete its life cycle.
organic: Remove nearby Juniper/Red Cedar trees if possible.
Plant rust-resistant apple varieties.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.description: Characterized by reddish-brown pustules on both upper and lower leaf surfaces.
organic: Plant resistant hybrids.
Rotate crops with non-grass species.
chemical: Strobilurin or Triazole fungicides if infection is severe.

Potato___Early_blight.description: Early blight causes dark spots with concentric rings on older leaves.
organic: Practice 3-year crop rotation.
Avoid overhead irrigation to keep leaves dry.
Apply compost tea or Serenade (Bacillus subtilis).

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.description: Small, water-soaked spots on leaves that eventually turn brown and paper-like.
organic: Use pathogen-free seeds.
Apply copper-based organic sprays.
Avoid working in the garden when plants are wet.
chemical: Copper-mancozeb tank mix for better control.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.description: Spread by whiteflies, it causes severe stunting and upward curling of leaves.
organic: Use reflective mulches to deter whiteflies.
Install fine mesh screening in greenhouses.
Remove and destroy infected plants immediately.
chemical: Imidacloprid or other insecticides to control whitefly populations.

Tomato___healthy.description: The plant appears healthy with no visible signs of disease.
organic: Continue regular watering and balanced fertilization.
Monitor regularly for early signs of pests.
